agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering expedited courier service with a target 2-hour delivery window, premium pricing, and a conditional refund policy for missed windows. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal stakeholders.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery Fulfillment — covers order eligibility, express pickup assignment, SLA tracking, real-time driver tracking, driver reassignment, and express fee refund processing.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium-tier delivery option with a target 2-hour delivery window from order placement, available only to eligible customers within a defined radius of a warehouse." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "The $12.99 surcharge for selecting express delivery, separate from the base delivery fee." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery fee charged on all orders regardless of delivery tier. Never refunded under the express SLA policy." },
      { "term": "Express Window", "definition": "The 2-hour target timeframe from order placement within which the delivery should be completed." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute maximum time from order placement for a driver to physically pick up the express order from the warehouse." },
      { "term": "Warehouse Radius", "definition": "The 10km maximum distance between the customer's delivery address and the nearest warehouse, used as an eligibility gate for express delivery." },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or above, qualified to be assigned express delivery orders." },
      { "term": "Driver Reassignment Window", "definition": "The 10-minute maximum time to assign a replacement driver after a mid-delivery driver cancellation on an express order." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "A refund of the express fee (not the base fee) issued when the express window is missed, framed as a goodwill gesture rather than a contractual obligation." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Exception", "definition": "A refund exclusion condition where the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, voiding the express window refund eligibility." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express delivery option is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — beyond 10km the logistics network cannot reliably fulfill the 2-hour window. Offering express outside this radius would be systematically deceptive.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a customer address >10km from all warehouses, the express delivery option MUST NOT be selectable at checkout."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can change with market conditions or promotional decisions.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a customer selects express delivery, the order total includes a line item of exactly $12.99 labeled as the express fee, separate from the base delivery fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "An express order must be picked up from the warehouse within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 15-minute threshold is an operational policy tuned to protect the 2-hour window. It could be adjusted if operational capabilities change.",
      "testable_assertion": "If no driver picks up the express order within 15 minutes of placement, the system flags the order as 'pickup SLA breached' and triggers an escalation."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above may be assigned express delivery orders.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Rating threshold is an operational quality gate that could be adjusted based on driver pool availability or performance data.",
      "testable_assertion": "When assigning a driver to an express order, the system MUST reject any driver with a rating below 4.0 stars."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When the express delivery window is missed, only the express fee ($12.99) is refunded — never the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "The base delivery fee covers actual fulfillment cost. Refunding it would mean the company absorbs the entire cost of a completed delivery. This is a financial integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a missed-window refund is issued, the refund amount equals exactly the express fee and the base delivery fee remains charged."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No express fee refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Moral hazard / fraud prevention — the customer caused the delay. Refunding would create a perverse incentive.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order with a missed window AND a 'wrong address' flag, the system MUST NOT issue an express fee refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "Express fee refunds for missed windows are processed within 3 business days.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Processing time is an operational SLA that can be adjusted based on finance team capacity or payment processor capabilities.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a missed-window refund is approved, it must be processed (submitted to payment provider) within 3 business days of approval."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-delivery on an express order and no replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes, the express fee is automatically refunded.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 10-minute window and auto-refund are customer success policies, adjustable by business decision.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order where the driver cancels mid-delivery and 10 minutes elapse with no replacement assigned, the system automatically initiates an express fee refund without manual intervention."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "Express customers must have access to real-time driver location tracking from pickup through delivery.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Real-time tracking is a product feature expectation, not a data integrity or physical constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "From the moment an express order driver picks up the package until delivery completion, the customer-facing tracking interface shows driver location updated at least every 30 seconds."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from a warehouse (boundary condition)", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — is the 10km radius inclusive or exclusive? Must be specified. Recommend inclusive (<=10km)." },
    { "scenario": "No express-eligible drivers (4+ stars) are available when an express order is placed", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — should the system reject the express order at checkout, queue it and risk breaching the 15-min pickup SLA, or degrade to a lower-rated driver? Must be defined." },
    { "scenario": "Pickup SLA (15 min) is breached but the delivery still completes within 2 hours", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — is this a success or a tracked operational failure? The pickup SLA breach should be logged as an ops incident, but the customer-facing SLA was met so no refund is triggered." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels mid-delivery, replacement assigned at minute 9, but replacement then also cancels", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does a second 10-minute window start? Or does the cumulative delay trigger an immediate refund? Chain-cancellation policy is undefined." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address mid-delivery to a location >10km from warehouse", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does the express SLA still apply? Does this count as a 'wrong address' exception? Address change policy during express fulfillment is undefined." },
    { "scenario": "Wrong address is disputed by the customer who claims the address was correct", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — who determines 'wrong address'? What is the evidence standard? What is the dispute resolution process? No arbiter or criteria defined." },
    { "scenario": "Express order placed at 11:50 PM — 2-hour window spans midnight / crosses business day boundary", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does the express window apply 24/7? Are there operating hours? If refund is needed, does the 3-business-day clock start on the next business day?" },
    { "scenario": "Warehouse goes offline (system outage, natural disaster) after express order is accepted", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — force majeure / system failure handling for express orders is not defined. Should auto-refund, auto-downgrade, or manual triage?" },
    { "scenario": "Customer places multiple express orders simultaneously", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — are multiple concurrent express orders allowed? Each requires a dedicated express-eligible driver, which could exhaust the pool." },
    { "scenario": "Driver rating drops below 4.0 mid-delivery (e.g., from a parallel order's review)", "expected_behavior": "The in-progress express delivery should continue — rating check is an assignment-time gate, not a continuous condition. But this needs explicit confirmation." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99) creates a new revenue stream but carries refund liability. Missed-window refunds, auto-refunds on driver cancellations, and the 3-business-day processing window create cash flow implications. The wrong-address exception is a critical fraud gate — without it, customers could game free express delivery. The contradiction between 'guarantee' and 'goodwill' language directly affects refund volume: a guarantee sets customer expectations of automatic refunds, while goodwill framing gives the company discretion.",
    "operational": "15-minute pickup SLA and 4-star driver requirement significantly constrain the available driver pool. Express orders will compete with standard orders for high-rated drivers. The 10-minute reassignment window for cancellations requires either a standing reserve of express-eligible drivers or a very responsive dispatch system. Real-time tracking adds infrastructure cost (GPS polling, WebSocket connections).",
    "user": "Customers get a premium, time-sensitive service with real-time visibility. However, the legal/PM contradiction on 'guarantee' vs 'best effort' will directly shape customer trust. If marketed as a guarantee but legally disclaimed, customer satisfaction will suffer when refunds are denied. Clear expectation-setting at purchase is critical.",
    "risk": "HIGH — the guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is a reputational and potential regulatory risk. Advertising a 'guarantee' while legally disclaiming it could be seen as misleading in some jurisdictions. The wrong-address exception lacks a clear adjudication process, creating dispute risk. The absence of operating hours, force majeure policy, and chain-cancellation handling creates operational blind spots.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "New premium option with faster delivery and real-time tracking. Risk of frustration if 'guarantee' language sets expectations the refund policy cannot meet." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "Only 4+ star drivers qualify, creating a two-tier system. Express assignments may carry more pressure and accountability. Drivers below 4 stars are excluded from express earnings." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "Must support 15-minute pickup SLA — orders need priority picking/staging. May require dedicated express staging areas." },
      { "group": "Finance/Accounting", "impact": "New refund processing workflow within 3 business days. Need to track express fee revenue vs. refund volume for margin analysis." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle wrong-address disputes, missed-window complaints, and expectation mismatches between 'guarantee' marketing and 'goodwill' refund reality." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must resolve guarantee vs. best-effort language before launch. Terms of service and marketing materials need alignment." },
      { "group": "Dispatch/Routing", "impact": "Must implement express-priority assignment logic, 15-min pickup monitoring, 10-min reassignment automation, and express-eligible driver pool management." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 38,
    "reasoning": "Confidence is critically low due to a fundamental contradiction between stakeholders on the core product promise. The PM and Customer Success describe a 'guarantee' while Legal explicitly says it cannot be a guarantee. This is not a minor terminology issue — it changes the entire product's contractual nature, refund policy framing, marketing language, and system behavior (automatic vs. discretionary refunds). Additionally, multiple operational edge cases (operating hours, force majeure, chain cancellations, wrong-address adjudication, no-driver-available scenarios) are completely undefined. The spec is too raw to produce reliable acceptance criteria.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount ($12.99) — all stakeholders agree",
      "10km warehouse radius eligibility gate — uncontested",
      "Base delivery fee is never refunded — uncontested",
      "15-minute pickup SLA — operationally justified",
      "4-star driver minimum — uncontested",
      "Wrong address voids refund — uncontested"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA — fundamental contradiction",
      "Whether refunds are automatic or discretionary — depends on guarantee vs. goodwill resolution",
      "Operating hours for express delivery — completely undefined",
      "Wrong address determination process — no criteria or arbiter defined",
      "Chain-cancellation policy — undefined",
      "What happens when no express-eligible drivers are available — undefined",
      "How pickup SLA breach interacts with the customer-facing delivery window — undefined"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not pickup time — PM implied this but it was not explicitly stated",
      "The 10km radius is measured as straight-line distance, not driving distance — unspecified",
      "Driver rating is the platform's aggregate rating, not a per-category or recent-window rating — unspecified",
      "The 4-star minimum is >= 4.0, not > 4.0 — unspecified",
      "Express delivery operates during the same hours as standard delivery — no hours mentioned at all",
      "Only one refund trigger can apply per order (missed window OR failed reassignment, not both) — unspecified"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "CRITICAL: PM and Customer Success describe the 2-hour delivery window as a 'guarantee' that should be 'shown prominently,' while Legal explicitly states it cannot be a guarantee in writing and must be framed as 'best effort' with refunds as a 'goodwill gesture.' This is a foundational contradiction that affects the entire product's contractual framework, marketing language, refund policy automation, and customer expectation setting. The system cannot be correctly designed until this is resolved.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Legal", "Customer Success", "Marketing", "Customers", "Finance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Frame as guarantee — accept legal risk, make refunds automatic and unconditional (minus wrong-address exception), market as '2-Hour Guarantee'",
        "B: Frame as best effort — refunds are discretionary goodwill, marketing says 'Express: targeting 2-hour delivery', no automatic refunds",
        "C: Hybrid — internal SLA target with automatic refund processing (to match guarantee-like experience) but legal terms frame it as best-effort with goodwill refunds. Marketing uses softer language like '2-Hour Express Target'"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — this is industry-standard for premium logistics tiers. Internally enforce it like a guarantee (auto-refunds, SLA tracking, ops accountability) while legally framing it as best-effort with goodwill. Marketing should use 'Express 2-Hour Target' not 'Guarantee.' This gives customers a reliable experience without creating legal exposure. But this MUST be a joint PM + Legal decision."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The 'wrong address' refund exception has no defined adjudication process. Who determines the address was wrong? What evidence is required? What if the customer disputes it? Without clear criteria, this becomes a source of customer complaints and inconsistent support decisions.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customer Support", "Customers", "Legal"],
      "options": [
        "A: Driver marks 'wrong address' in-app with photo evidence, customer can dispute, support reviews",
        "B: System detects address mismatch (e.g., GPS at delivery point doesn't match entered address), automated determination",
        "C: Any address correction requested by customer mid-delivery automatically flags the order as 'customer-caused delay'"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A with B as supporting evidence — driver flags with photo, system cross-references GPS, support has both signals for dispute resolution."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "Operating hours for express delivery are completely undefined. Is express available 24/7? If not, what happens to orders placed near closing time where the 2-hour window would extend past operating hours?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Drivers", "Warehouse Operations"],
      "options": [
        "A: Express is 24/7 — requires round-the-clock warehouse and driver staffing",
        "B: Express has defined hours (e.g., 8 AM - 8 PM) — must prevent express selection outside hours",
        "C: Express availability is dynamic based on warehouse/driver capacity — system shows/hides option in real-time"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B for launch with a path to Option C — define fixed hours initially, evolve to dynamic availability as operational data accumulates."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No policy exists for what happens when no express-eligible drivers (4+ stars) are available at order time. This is a likely scenario during peak hours or in areas with limited driver supply.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Dispatch"],
      "options": [
        "A: Hide express option when no eligible drivers are available (dynamic eligibility)",
        "B: Accept the order and hope a driver becomes available within the 15-min pickup window",
        "C: Accept with a warning: 'Express drivers are in high demand, your order may be delayed'"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — do not sell what you cannot fulfill. Dynamic eligibility prevents systematic SLA breaches and refund liability."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The interaction between the 10-minute driver reassignment window auto-refund (Customer Success) and the wrong-address exception (Finance) is undefined. If a driver cancels mid-delivery because the address appears wrong, and no replacement is found in 10 minutes, does the auto-refund trigger or does the wrong-address exception block it?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customer Success", "Finance", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Wrong-address exception always takes priority — no auto-refund if address is flagged",
        "B: Auto-refund triggers regardless — wrong-address is only evaluated for window-miss refunds, not cancellation-reassignment refunds",
        "C: Driver cancellation reason determines path — if driver cancelled citing 'wrong address', exception applies; otherwise auto-refund"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — the driver's cancellation reason provides the signal. If they cancelled because the address was unreachable/wrong, that's a customer-caused issue. If they cancelled for personal reasons, the customer shouldn't be penalized."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
